{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''{{unicode|Šspt}}''}} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als [[Triangel]]-Dekan an dritter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit ist ihm das [[Horusauge]] zugeordnet, dem drei [[Stern]]e folgen. Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im Grab des [[Sethos I.]] an dritter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''{{unicode|Šspt}}''}} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als Vertreter der [[Dekanlisten C|Gruppe C]] sowie [[Triangel]]-Dekan an dritter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit ist ihm das [[Horusauge]] zugeordnet, dem drei [[Stern]]e folgen. Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im Grab des [[Sethos I.]] an dritter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

Als Gottheit ist ihm das [[Horusauge]] zugeordnet, dem drei [[Stern]]e folgen. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''{{unicode|Šspt}}''}} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Achet]]. In Rückrechnung hätte Schespet auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en des [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reichs]] als Dekanstern im zweiten Monat der [[Schemu]]-Jahreszeit die erste Position eingenommen. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als Vertreter der [[Dekanlisten C|Gruppe C]] sowie als [[Triangel]]-Dekan an dritter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit ist ihm das [[Horusauge]] zugeordnet, dem drei [[Stern]]e folgen. Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im Grab des [[Sethos I.]] an dritter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''{{unicode|Šspt}}'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomische Darstellungen im Grab des Senenmut
(Schespet ist im oberen Bild links oben zu sehen) }} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Achet]]. In Rückrechnung hätte Schespet auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en des [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reichs]] als Dekanstern des zweiten Monats der [[Schemu]]-Jahreszeit die erste Position eingenommen. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als Vertreter der [[Dekanlisten C|Gruppe C]] sowie als [[Triangel]]-Dekan mit dem [[Determinativ]] eines [[Stern|Einzelsterns]] an dritter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit sind dem Dekanstern die beiden [[Horusauge]]n zugeordnet, dem drei Sterne folgen. Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im Grab des [[Sethos I.]] an dritter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''{{unicode|Šspt}}'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomische Darstellungen im Grab des Senenmut
(Schespet ist im oberen Bild links oben zu sehen) }} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Achet]]. In Rückrechnung hätte Schespet auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en des [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reichs]] als Dekanstern die erste Position in der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|ersten Dekade des vierten Monats der Jahreszeit Achet]] eingenommen. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als Vertreter der [[Dekanlisten C|Gruppe C]] sowie als [[Triangel]]-Dekan mit dem [[Determinativ]] eines [[Stern|Einzelsterns]] an zweiter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit sind dem Dekanstern die beiden [[Horusauge]]n zugeordnet, denen drei Sterne folgen.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im [[KV17|Grab des Sethos I.]] an zweiter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Anmerkungen == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

Als Gottheit sind dem Dekanstern die beiden [[Horusauge]]n zugeordnet, denen drei Sterne folgen.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im [[KV17|Grab des Sethos I.]] an zweiter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Anmerkungen == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomische Darstellungen im Grab des Senenmut
(Schespet ist im oberen Bild links oben zu sehen) }} '''Schespet''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]]. In den [[Dekanlisten A2]], beispielsweise auf der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605]], stand Schespet zwischen [[Weschati-Bekati|Bekati]] und [[Tepi-a chentet]] an zehnter Position des ersten Monats der Jahreszeit [[Achet]]. In Rückrechnung hätte Schespet auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en des [[Mittleres Reich|Mittleren Reichs]] als Dekanstern die erste Position in der [[Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|ersten Dekade des vierten Monats der Jahreszeit Achet]] eingenommen. Im [[TT353|Grab des Senenmut]] steht Schespet als Vertreter der [[Dekanlisten C|Gruppe C]] sowie als [[Triangel]]-Dekan mit dem [[Determinativ]] eines [[Stern|Einzelsterns]] an zweiter Position der [[Heriu-renpet]]-Dekane. Als Gottheit sind dem Dekanstern die beiden [[Horusauge]]n zugeordnet, denen drei Sterne folgen.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Ebenfalls als Triangel- und Heriu-renpet-Dekan ist Schespet im [[KV17|Grab des Sethos I.]] an zweiter Position zu sehen, dort mit [[Horus]] und dem Horusauge im Gefolge. == Literatur == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, S. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Anmerkungen == [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Schespet]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Schespet]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Schespet]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:シェスペト]]

In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Horus Eye|de|Horusauge|lt=Horus eyes}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List c}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the [[Dekanlisten A2|Dekan Lists A2]], such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 pages]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:New]]

(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Schespet]][[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]][[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:11]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:New]]

(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr}}. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{draft categories| [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[sn:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[sn:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=draft|12=draft}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=draft|12=draft}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=draft|12=draft}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ|12=draft|12=draft}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=draft|12=draft}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I|12=draft|12=draft}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I|12=draft|12=draft}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|qq=draft}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|qq=draft}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ|qq=draft}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I|qq=draft}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category: in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[sn:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Schespet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Hieroglyphen" to "Infobox hieroglyphs"
revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Achet|de|Achet|qq=draft}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|de|Mittleres Reich|qq=draft}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|de|Determinativ|qq=draft}} of a {{ill|Star (Heraldry)|lt=single star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|KV17|de|KV17|lt=tomb of Seti I|qq=draft}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Schespet]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Schespet]] [[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[sn: failed]]

In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schespet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic ]] }} [[sn:Schespet]] [[en:draft:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]], Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schespet]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]], Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Schespet]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2233871]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]], Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. [[de:Schespet]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2233871]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]], Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}}

In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom (Egypt)]], Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the [[:en:Determinative|Determinative]] of a [[:en:Star|single star]] at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
