In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Achet]]. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two [[:en:Eye_of_Horus|Eyes of Horus]], followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the [[:en:Tomb of Seti I|tomb of Seti I]] at the second position, accompanied by [[:en:Horus|Horus]] and the Horus eye. == Literature == * [[:en:Jochem Kahl|Jochem Kahl]]: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom (Egypt)}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|en|Determinative|WD=Q1201172|lt=Determinative}} of a {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=single star}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Eye_of_Horus|en|Eye_of_Horus|WD=Q211286|lt=Eyes of Horus}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|Tomb of Seti I|en|Tomb of Seti I|WD=Q1420195|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * {{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom (Egypt)}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|en|Determinative|WD=Q1201172|lt=Determinative}} of a {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=single star}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Eye_of_Horus|en|Eye_of_Horus|WD=Q211286|lt=Eyes of Horus}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|Tomb of Seti I|en|Tomb of Seti I|WD=Q1420195|lt=tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * {{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483|1=Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet|1=Season of the Inundation}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201|1=Diagonal star clock EA47605}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|en|Determinative|WD=Q1201172|lt=Determinative|1=Determinative}} of a {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=single star|1=Star}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070|1=Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Eye_of_Horus|en|Eye_of_Horus|WD=Q211286|lt=Eyes of Horus|1=Eye of Horus}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|Tomb of Seti I|en|Tomb of Seti I|WD=Q1420195|lt=tomb of Seti I|1=Tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * {{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl|1=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

[[de:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] [[de:Schespet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O42-p:t-N14 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Schespet
''Šspt'' |BILD1 = Senenmut.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Astronomical depictions in the tomb of Senenmut
(Schespet is seen at the top left of the upper image) }} '''Schespet''' is the name of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} [[Dekan (Ancient Egypt)|decan]]. In the {{ill|8=de|9=Dekanlisten A2|A2 Decan Lists|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=de|qq=}}, such as on the [[Diagonal star clock EA47605|Diagonal Star Clock EA47605]], Schespet was positioned between {{ill|Weschati-Bekati|de|Weschati-Bekati|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weschati-Bekati|qq=|WD=Q1339483|1=Weschati-Bekati}} and {{ill|Tepi-a chentet|de|Tepi-a chentet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tepi-a chentet|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} at the tenth position of the first month of the season {{ill|Season of the Inundation|en|Season of the Inundation|WD=Q770568|lt=Achet|1=Season of the Inundation}}. Retrospectively, on the diagonal star clocks of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom (Egypt)|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Schespet would have been the decan star occupying the first position in the {{ill|Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|lt=first decade of the fourth month of the season Achet|de|Diagonalsternuhr EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock EA47605#Dekanverteilung auf der Diagonalsternuhr|qq=|WD=Q1208201|1=Diagonal star clock EA47605}}. In the {{ill|TT353|de|Grab des Senenmut|lt=tomb of Senenmut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Schespet represents the {{ill|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|de|Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|2=de|3=Dekanlisten C|1=Decan List C|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dekanlisten C|13=User:Immanuelle/Decan List C|qq=|WD=Q1183637}} group and appears as a triangle-decan marked with the {{ill|Determinative|en|Determinative|WD=Q1201172|lt=Determinative|1=Determinative}} of a {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=single star|1=Star}} at the second position of the {{ill|Heriu-renpet|de|Heriu-renpet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heriu-renpet|qq=|WD=Q1610070|1=Heriu-renpet}} decans. As a deity, the decan star is associated with the two {{ill|Eye_of_Horus|en|Eye_of_Horus|WD=Q211286|lt=Eyes of Horus|1=Eye of Horus}}, followed by three stars.D4:D4-G5-N14:N14:N14 Schespet also appears as a triangle and Heriu-renpet decan in the {{ill|Tomb of Seti I|en|Tomb of Seti I|WD=Q1420195|lt=tomb of Seti I|1=Tomb of Seti I}} at the second position, accompanied by {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|WD=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} and the Horus eye. == Literature == * {{ill|Jochem Kahl|en|Jochem Kahl|WD=Q1691027|lt=Jochem Kahl|1=Jochem Kahl}}: ''Siut-Theben: Zur Wertschätzung von Traditionen im Alten Ägypten''. Brill, Leiden 1999, ISBN 9-0041-1441-6 * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9 * Sarah Symons: ''Two fragments of diagonal star clocks in the British Museum''. Journal for the History of Astronomy Vol. 33, Part 3, Nr. 112, Leicester 2002, pp. 257–260. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Schespet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Schespet]] [[ja:シェスペト]] {{wikidata link|Q2233871}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
